mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.0% and remains there for at least 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 1.82% suggests easing environment
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS < 2.0% trigger are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) compared to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payroll employment of more than 100,000 jobs signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reduce equity exposure.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 100,000 persons in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 148,912 thousand shows stable employment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts measured by HOUST combined with accelerating retail sales measured by RSAFS indicate robust consumer demand and capital investment, jointly driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases by more than 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS increases by more than 3% month-over-month in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1,350 thousand and RSAFS at 501,971 million show moderate demand
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: The joint signal (both HOUST and RSAFS rising simultaneously) fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production measured by INDPRO signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as profit margin compression becomes evident.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months with cumulative decline exceeding 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 107.3006 shows recent stability
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following INDPRO decline trigger are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 3.5% combined with the federal funds rate remaining below 2.5% creates a negative real rate environment that erodes bond returns and forces investors into equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 3.5% AND FEDFUNDS is below 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 250.857 and FEDFUNDS at 1.82% suggest negative real rates
failure_condition: The joint condition (high inflation + low rates) fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate measured by UNRATE rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending power and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% and remains elevated for at least 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.0% shows tight labor market
regime_hint: labor market deterioration and recession risk
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following UNRATE > 4.5% trigger are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 accelerating above 2.5% annualized rate combined with rising retail sales measured by RSAFS indicates robust economic expansion that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth exceeds 2.5% AND RSAFS shows positive year-over-year growth above 3%
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 17,371.854 billion and RSAFS at 501,971 million show moderate growth
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion with synchronized growth
failure_condition: The joint signal (strong GDP growth + rising sales) fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production measured by INDPRO accelerating above 2.0% year-over-year combined with declining unemployment measured by UNRATE below 4.0% signals a tight supply-demand balance that supports corporate pricing power and margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth exceeds 2.0% AND UNRATE remains below 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 107.3006 and UNRATE at 4.0% show tight supply-labor conditions
regime_hint: tight supply-demand balance with pricing power
failure_condition: The joint condition (strong production + tight labor) fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
